descripcion:
One trip to Italy is never enough. @StanleyTucci returns for a second season of #TucciinItaly to explore 5 new regions, diving deeper into the food, people and traditions that define Italy. 

Don't miss the all-new season of #TucciInItaly, premiering May 11 at 9/8c on @natgeotv. Streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu.

This Earth Month, step into wonder with National Geographic as we celebrate everything that makes our planet so extraordinary 🌍

Stream the National Geographic #EarthMonth collection all April long on @DisneyPlus. #StepIntoWonder

You know bees make honey, but do you know exactly how they transform watery, sugary nectar into thick, shelf-stable honey?

Hosted by @bertiegregory, #SecretsOfTheBees is now streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu
Red pandas are super cute, super stinky, and also not really pandas—having more in common with raccoons and weasels. Discover five facts about these adorable tree-top dwellers.

Bats and a spectacular sunset make for an unforgettable combo.

#HostilePlanet is now streaming on @DisneyPlus.
Photo by National Geographic on April 18, 2026. May be an image of text.

For National Geographic Explorer Hana Raza, moments of wonder can come at the most surprising times—like when her camera trap photographed a Persian leopard, an animal thought lost in Iraq's Zagros Mountains. @insidenatgeo

#StepIntoWonder with National Geographic’s #EarthMonth collection throughout April on @DisneyPlus.

To celebrate Earth Month, Maejor—a multi-platinum artist and producer—partnered with National Geographic for his new album Earth Moods: Frequencies (Vol. 1). With its emphasis on supporting energetic alignment, this track matches the wonder of Iceland’s striking yet serene landscape.

Listen to @maejor's album wherever you stream music now, and #StepIntoWonder with our #EarthMoods livestream throughout April on Nat Geo's YouTube channel at the link in bio.

When we get conservation right, it can lead to moments that take your breath away. That's what @bertiegregory experienced while filming the largest gathering of fin whales. 

#StepIntoWonder with National Geographic’s Earth Month collection and stream #EpicAdventuresWithBertieGregory on @DisneyPlus.

Photos by Reuben Wu @reuben | From Punta Helbronner, 11,370 feet (3,466 meters) above sea level on the Italian side of Mont Blanc in the Alps, my guide walks out across the snow toward Dent du Géant, the “Giant’s Tooth,” illuminated only by the light of my drone during a long exposure.

In images like this, I’m interested in how a small change in the light can shift the scale of a landscape, briefly turning a lone figure into a point of orientation within something far larger and older.

In the blue hour, the mountain held its shape with a seemingly absolute stillness, while that moving light traced a human presence across the snow for just a few seconds.

For more visual stories, follow me @reuben.
